theme: Skia m151 and allocation-free hot paths
highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 preview updates to Skia m151 and eliminates per-call allocations across SKShaper and HarfBuzzSharp.
highlights_body: The bundled Skia engine advances to m151 with upstream bug fixes. A series of targeted performance improvements make SKFourByteTag parsing, SKColor hex parsing, and SKShaper glyph access allocation-free. HarfBuzzSharp gains pooled UTF-8 encoding and zero-allocation tag parsing. A WASM build compatibility fix is also included.

bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine advances to m151, bringing upstream rendering improvements and bug fixes.
Additional cherry-picked fixes from the Skia chrome/m151 branch have been merged for improved stability.
A redefined SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM preprocessor symbol that broke WASM builds has been removed from the Skia submodule.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing optimised with ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char>, and the internal hex-to-byte conversion avoids allocations on the hot path.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph results exposed as zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: SKShaper now surfaces glyph result arrays as ReadOnlySpan slices, eliminating the per-call array copy when reading shaping output.
SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocates a temporary char[4] array on every call; the four-byte tag is decoded directly from the integer value.
The unnecessary unsafe modifier has been removed from GRContextOptions, making the type usable in projects that do not allow unsafe code.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Fixed the WASM SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM preprocessor redefinition and added a cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness

preview_summaries.4.151.0-p1: Preview 1 updated the bundled Skia engine to milestone m151, restored WASM build compatibility, added SKColor ReadOnlySpan overloads and SKShaper zero-copy span access, and made SKFourByteTag parsing allocation-free.

harfbuzz_summary: Two allocation-free performance improvements: Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled UTF-8 encoder to avoid per-call byte[] allocation, and Tag.Parse no longer allocates a temporary char[4] array.
